core_model.structure_summary: A set of interacting positive scalars evolves under coupled stochastic differential equations with state-dependent noise and a deterministic drift containing pairwise logarithmic-repulsion terms of the form (x_a x_b)/(x_a - x_b), constrained to a simplex.
core_model.canonical_form: dx_\alpha = 4\gamma\Big[\sum_{\beta\neq\alpha}\frac{x_\alpha x_\beta}{x_\alpha-x_\beta} + h(\vec x)\Big]dt + 2\sqrt{\gamma}\,x_\alpha\Big(dB_\alpha - \sum_\beta x_\beta dB_\beta\Big);\qquad \partial_t P = \tfrac{\Gamma}{2}\partial_\omega\big[(\partial_\omega + 2V'(\omega))P\big]

variables.meaning: collection of interacting positive scalars (spectrum) summing to one
independent Brownian increments, state-multiplied and mean-subtracted
monitoring/dephasing rates controlling drift and noise strength

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A gradient flow of interacting scalars down a potential combining pairwise logarithmic repulsion between all pairs and a confining single-body term, whose stationary state is the Coulomb-gas / equilibrium density.

notes: Core is a Dyson-Brownian-motion-type coupled SDE for the spectrum with multiplicative (state-dependent) noise and log-repulsion drift; equivalently a Fokker-Planck equation for the density. Change of variables recasts the drift as minus-gradient of a potential V with log-sinh pairwise repulsion plus a confining term, so the deterministic skeleton is the corresponding gradient flow / Coulomb-gas equilibrium. Normalization (trace one) is structural.
